name: Stuffed Pork Chops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor or blender, process the apples, 4 tablespoons of butter, brown sugar, vanilla, and nutmeg until smooth.
2. Set aside. Thoroughly combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl; set aside.
3. Prepare the pork chops by cutting a large pocket (to the bone) into the larger side of each chop to hold the stuffing.
4. In a large skillet, brown the ground pork in the remaining 3 tablespoons butter over high heat for about 3 minutes.
5. Add onions, bell peppers, garlic, and 2 teaspoons of the seasoning mix; stir well and cook about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping pan bottom well.
6. Stir in green chilies and their juice; continue cooking until mixture is well browned, about 6 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping pan bottom as needed.
7. Add stock and cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
8. Stir in bread crumbs and cook about 2 more minutes, stirring constantly and scraping pan bottom as needed.
9. Remove from heat. Sprinkle the remaining seasoning mix evenly on both sides of the chops and inside pockets; press it in by hand.
10. Arrange chops with pocket side up in an ungreased baking pan. Spoon about 1/4 cup stuffing into each pocket; reserve the remaining stuffing.
11. Bake at 400°F until meat is done, about 1 hour 10 minutes.
12. Place remaining stuffing in a small pan and continue reheating for the last 20 minutes.
13. Serve immediately with chops arranged on top of a portion of the remaining stuffing.
